title: Soda Tax

jokes.text: "Box 1 of 12"?
jokes.setup: Ben opens what seems like a normal care package with sweet items
jokes.punchline: The reveal that this is just the first of twelve boxes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that subverts expectations - what seemed sweet becomes overwhelming. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: "I've been wearing a bandana as underwear for three days now."
jokes.setup: Andy's request for April to do his laundry seems lazy
jokes.punchline: The disgusting reveal of his improvised underwear solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - gross, childish, but somehow endearing. Highly quotable and specific to his character.

jokes.text: Look, there's a picture. Ah! That's horrifying.
jokes.setup: Andy mentions wearing a bandana as underwear
jokes.punchline: April's horrified reaction to photographic evidence
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up beat that extends the previous joke's impact

jokes.text: I love him so much.
jokes.setup: April just expressed horror at Andy's disgusting behavior
jokes.punchline: Immediate declaration of love despite being horrified
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - captures April's deadpan delivery and the contradictory nature of love. Great economy and structure.

jokes.text: What did you put in the sugar? It's so good.
jokes.setup: Leslie is showing off gross sugar consumption while arguing against soda
jokes.punchline: She's secretly enjoying eating the prop sugar
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy moment - Leslie's addiction to sugar undermining her health campaign

jokes.text: Paunch Burger, Big and Wide, The Fat Sack, Coronel Plum's Slop Trough
jokes.setup: Leslie listing local restaurants to help get healthier
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous and unappetizing restaurant names
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdist naming convention, builds a comedic world

jokes.text: which was formerly Sue's Salads until we ran that out of town
jokes.setup: Leslie listing unhealthy restaurants she wants to help
jokes.punchline: Casual admission they drove the one healthy option out of business
jokes.explanation: Dark irony that reveals the town's backwards priorities while Leslie remains oblivious

jokes.text: Number two is right.
jokes.setup: Ron orders a "Number two" burger meal
jokes.punchline: Leslie's bathroom humor response to the gross food
jokes.explanation: Simple poop joke with good economy but predictable and not very clever

jokes.text: Get me a refill.
jokes.setup: Leslie's emotional speech about how Ron has supported her and asking how to repay him
jokes.punchline: Ron's completely practical, unemotional response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - deflects emotion with practical request. Great character integration and economy.

jokes.text: definitely no Papyrus
jokes.setup: Ben lecturing about consistent fonts in professional memos
jokes.punchline: Singling out the notoriously bad Papyrus font
jokes.explanation: Good nerd humor that fits Ben's character - specific enough to be funny to design-aware viewers

jokes.text: Papyrus? Are you kidding me? There's no place for that in a professional office setting.
jokes.setup: Ben mentions Papyrus font
jokes.punchline: April unexpectedly passionate about font choices despite not caring about work
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - April caring about something professional when she usually doesn't care about anything

jokes.text: they call me Devo, 'cause I can "whip 'em good."
jokes.setup: Ben talking about training college interns
jokes.punchline: Awkward 80s band reference as a nickname
jokes.explanation: Mildly cringey pun that fits Ben's awkward attempts to be cool with younger people

jokes.text: 13's just obnoxious.
jokes.setup: Ben specifying 12-point font
jokes.punchline: His strong opinion about 13-point being obnoxious
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific obsession with minor details that most people wouldn't notice

jokes.text: Community college. Eh, that's too far.
jokes.setup: Someone asks where Jerry and others are working out
jokes.punchline: Jerry's lazy excuse for not exercising
jokes.explanation: Typical Jerry laziness - simple but effective character moment

jokes.text: Andy demonstrates his one push-up ability
jokes.setup: Andy claiming he's getting in shape for police academy
jokes.punchline: Visual of his pathetic single push-up demonstration
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing Andy's terrible physical condition despite his optimism

jokes.text: That's a typo, right? I mean, that's humanly impossible.
jokes.setup: Andy reading requirement to run 2 miles in under 25 minutes
jokes.punchline: Thinking this extremely easy pace is impossible
jokes.explanation: Good character ignorance - Andy's complete lack of understanding about basic fitness

jokes.text: Oh, do you mean the "li'I swallow"?
jokes.setup: Discussion about smaller soda size options
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous name for the smallest size
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist product naming that builds the satirical world of corporate food

jokes.text: Some girls buy them for their dollhouses, but they're not very popular.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the tiny "li'l swallow" soda size
jokes.punchline: The only customers are children for dollhouses
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd sizing, makes the corporate logic even more ridiculous

jokes.text: Most people call it a gallon, but they call it the "regular."
jokes.setup: Discussing Paunch Burger's 128-ounce option
jokes.punchline: A full gallon being called "regular" size
jokes.explanation: Great satirical escalation showing how corporations manipulate size perceptions

jokes.text: that they call "child size."
jokes.setup: Leslie describing a 512-ounce soda
jokes.punchline: This massive drink being called "child size"
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation with dark implications about marketing to children

jokes.text: Well, it's roughly the size of a two-year-old child, if the child were liquefied.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking how 512 ounces could be called "child size"
jokes.punchline: The horrifying literal explanation of the naming
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark absurdist moment - takes corporate doublespeak to its logical horrifying conclusion. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: No! I'm never gonna be a cop. I'm gonna have to be a robber.
jokes.setup: Andy failing his fitness test for police academy
jokes.punchline: His childish logic about becoming a criminal instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - binary thinking with childish reasoning. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Not because of what you did, which was terrible, but because of what I'm going to be able to get you to do.
jokes.setup: Chris saying he's proud of Andy
jokes.punchline: Clarifying it's not for his performance but for future potential
jokes.explanation: Good backhanded compliment that fits Chris's relentlessly positive but honest nature

jokes.text: I actually think you look really good, except for the stick up your butt. Who do you think drew it?
jokes.setup: Ben asking about inappropriate drawings of him
jokes.punchline: April's casual admission while seemingly complimenting the artwork
jokes.explanation: Good April moment - disguising insult as compliment with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Nathaniel is related to Donald Rumsfeld. Brittany's dad is Ben Bernanke's dentist.
jokes.setup: Ben learning the interns are well-connected
jokes.punchline: Increasingly tenuous but powerful connections
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how D.C. networking works through increasingly ridiculous connections

jokes.text: The zero on the label refers to the amount of water in it, which is zero.
jokes.setup: Leslie questioning why "water zero" has 300 calories
jokes.punchline: The absurd corporate logic explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant corporate doublespeak that parodies misleading product naming

jokes.text: If you want zero-calorie water, try diet water zero lite. It has only 60 calories.
jokes.setup: Discussion of misleading water product names
jokes.punchline: Even the diet version contradicts its own name
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that extends the absurd corporate naming logic

jokes.text: Consequently, we haven't had sex in ten years. I-I thought you said he gained weight in the last three months. Well, we have lots of other problems.
jokes.setup: Woman blaming soda for lack of sex due to husband's weight gain
jokes.punchline: Timeline doesn't add up, revealing deeper marital issues
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that reveals the real problems aren't about soda

jokes.text: All taxation is theft! If the government can tax me, I-I can--I can do this. Grover, give that woman her purse back.
jokes.setup: Man arguing against taxation at town hall
jokes.punchline: He steals a purse to prove his point, gets caught immediately
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of anti-government logic taken to criminal extreme

jokes.text: Orange racing stripe. It's a perfect recreation of Han lue's Nissan in The Fast And The Furious: Tokyo Drift. Weirdly, so far, no one has noticed.
jokes.setup: Tom showing off his modified pace car
jokes.punchline: His disappointment that no one recognizes his niche reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's obsession with pop culture references others don't care about

jokes.text: I exercise because, when I was a baby, I had a rare blood disorder, and I suppose I need to feel like my body's in tip-top shape... So it doesn't destroy me, leaving me to die alone.
jokes.setup: Chris explaining his motivation for exercise
jokes.punchline: His deep existential fear beneath the positive exterior
jokes.explanation: Good character depth moment - reveals the darkness behind Chris's relentless positivity

jokes.text: what's up, my male? You grab a slice of 'za, brah.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to connect with young interns
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at youth slang
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Ben's desperate attempts to seem cool

jokes.text: We should lock down some tight disc grabs, am I right, ell-bones?
jokes.setup: Ben continuing to try connecting with Ellis
jokes.punchline: Making up terrible nicknames and using frisbee jargon wrong
jokes.explanation: Continues Ben's awkward attempts but less effective than earlier moments

jokes.text: I think we should tax all bad things, like racism and women's vaginae.
jokes.setup: Town hall discussion about what to tax
jokes.punchline: Citizen's completely inappropriate and illogical suggestions
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that's more shock value than clever - relies on inappropriate content

jokes.text: We're not taxing anyone's genitals. Then what the hell are we doing here?
jokes.setup: Leslie clarifying they won't tax genitals
jokes.punchline: Citizen's confusion about the meeting's purpose
jokes.explanation: Follow-up to previous joke but doesn't add much value

jokes.text: Maybe instead of me losing my job, you ought to lose your job. Let's recall Leslie Knope. Let's do this!
jokes.setup: Worker complaining about potential job loss
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to recalling Leslie
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how quickly town halls can turn hostile

jokes.text: Someone please tell me we kodaked that moment.
jokes.setup: Ben making a good frisbee catch
jokes.punchline: Using outdated photography term
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's age through outdated slang

jokes.text: All right, April, what's the score? 1,000 to 7.
jokes.setup: Ben asking for the Ultimate frisbee score
jokes.punchline: April's completely impossible and deadpan score report
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - absurd but delivered with complete deadpan confidence

jokes.text: Nobody. Nothing. Does it really even matter?
jokes.setup: Andy asking what Chris runs for
jokes.punchline: Chris's existential crisis showing through his usually positive facade
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Chris's deeper issues breaking through his positive exterior

jokes.text: There's something wrong with my body. My legs aren't working. I'm broken.
jokes.setup: Andy after minimal exercise
jokes.punchline: His dramatic overreaction to normal muscle fatigue
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment showing his childish reaction to minor discomfort

jokes.text: I'm floating in this giant river of ambiguity. I'm under a warm waterfall of uncertainty.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing her confused state about voting
jokes.punchline: Her overly poetic and mixed metaphors
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment showing her tendency toward overly dramatic metaphors

jokes.text: Do you just have to pee real bad? Yes.
jokes.setup: Leslie's poetic descriptions of uncertainty
jokes.punchline: Ann's practical explanation for Leslie's discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that deflates Leslie's dramatic metaphors with practical reality

jokes.text: Oh, no.
jokes.setup: Leslie's name being called for her vote
jokes.punchline: Her realization she still has to pee during the crucial moment
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that extends the bathroom humor setup

jokes.text: Mayhaps, might I suggest that we have a re--aah!
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to speak formally while in distress
jokes.punchline: Her attempt at fancy language interrupted by physical discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie trying to maintain dignity while in distress

jokes.text: Do you guys know how to dissolve super glue?
jokes.setup: Doctor treating Leslie and Andy
jokes.punchline: Casual question about unrelated emergency
jokes.explanation: Minor background gag suggesting other medical mishaps happening

jokes.text: Ell-bow. Ell-chupacabra. Drinkin' coffee.
jokes.setup: Ben continuing to try making nicknames for Ellis
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate and nonsensical nickname attempts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ben's increasingly desperate attempts to connect

jokes.text: it's from 40 years ago. Never mind.
jokes.setup: Ben making an outdated reference
jokes.punchline: His realization that he's showing his age
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment about the generation gap

jokes.text: Hey, man, I didn't draw it. Your daughter did. What? Her. April's not my daughter. She's my friend.
jokes.setup: Ben confronting Ellis about disrespectful drawings
jokes.punchline: Ellis revealing April drew them, Ben's confusion about the relationship
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shifts blame and reveals age gap confusion

jokes.text: Sorry, Dad.
jokes.setup: Ben insisting April would never disrespect him
jokes.punchline: April's sarcastic 'Dad' acknowledgment while admitting guilt
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - maximum mischief with minimum words, great callback timing

jokes.text: So what? I've tried to fire you.
jokes.setup: Leslie worried about people wanting to fire her
jokes.punchline: Ron's casual admission of his own attempts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron delivery - brutal honesty delivered with complete nonchalance

jokes.text: No, no, no, no. I tried to fire you four times.
jokes.setup: Leslie shocked that Ron tried to fire her once
jokes.punchline: Ron escalating to reveal four attempts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes Ron's honesty even more brutal

jokes.text: They found nothing. Nothing? Nothing. The silent killer.
jokes.setup: Chris getting medical test results
jokes.punchline: Treating perfect health as a terrifying diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing Chris's health anxiety taken to illogical extreme

jokes.text: I'm gonna die an anonymous, meaningless speck of dust.
jokes.setup: Chris having an existential crisis
jokes.punchline: His dramatic description of mortality fears
jokes.explanation: Continues Chris's character arc but less impactful than earlier moments

jokes.text: It's too bad there's not doctors for your mind.
jokes.setup: Chris having mental health issues
jokes.punchline: Andy's innocent ignorance of therapists/psychiatrists
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his innocent ignorance about mental health care

jokes.text: I need to climb the Mount Everest of my mind.
jokes.setup: Chris talking about internal goals vs external ones
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic metaphor for therapy
jokes.explanation: Typical Chris over-the-top metaphor but not as strong as his other moments

jokes.text: Right. That was the worst thing you were.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying she was a dedicated public servant
jokes.punchline: Ron confirming that was her worst quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - turning a positive trait into an insult through his anti-government perspective

jokes.text: 12%. 15. For God's sake, I'm asking for 15% effort. It's not supposed to be a negotiation.
jokes.setup: Ben asking April for 15% effort
jokes.punchline: April trying to negotiate down to 12%, Ben's exasperation
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing April's laziness and Ben's reasonable desperation

jokes.text: Ellis hates you, and he has herpes.
jokes.setup: Ben asking about Ellis
jokes.punchline: April's casually brutal revelation about Ellis
jokes.explanation: Good April moment but the herpes addition feels like cheap shock value

jokes.text: I'm gonna get a melon baller and scoop your eyes out and eat them, and your congressman uncle is gonna have to buy you a dog to drag your eyeless face around.
jokes.setup: April threatening Ellis to do his work
jokes.punchline: Her extremely graphic and specific threat
jokes.explanation: Classic April moment - extremely dark and specific threat delivered matter-of-factly. Highly quotable and memorable.

tmdb_overview: Leslie decides to wage war on obesity in Pawnee in her first mission as city councilor by passing a city-wide soda tax. Meanwhile, Andy tries to get in shape for his police-entrance exam, and Ben struggles to connect with the interns in his office.
insight: Solid character work carries a mid-tier episode through policy-debate tedium.
